winner.title: Off-chain session token format with JWT-compatible envelope
winner.description: Define a standardized off-chain session token that wraps a verified handshake result into a JWT-compatible envelope (RFC 7519). After on-chain verifyHandshake succeeds, the relayer issues a signed JWT whose claims include humanNullifier, agentNullifier, scopeCommitment, and sessionNonce — enabling stateless verification by downstream services without additional on-chain calls. The token MUST include an `iss` claim bound to the relayer's DID, a `nbf`/`exp` window, and a `bolyra_proof_tx` claim linking back to the on-chain settlement. This bridges the gap between Bolyra's on-chain verification and the HTTP-native auth flows that web services expect.

outline.title: Off-chain session token format with JWT-compatible envelope

artifacts.description: Normative specification for the Bolyra off-chain session token: JWT claims registry (humanNullifier, agentNullifier, scopeCommitment, sessionNonce, bolyra_proof_tx), iss/nbf/exp/jti requirements, signing algorithm constraints (ES256K / EdDSA), and token lifecycle rules.
Amend to add relayer DID document structure: verificationMethod entries that downstream verifiers use to resolve the iss claim and validate the JWT signature.

issueSessionToken(handshakeResult, relayerPrivKey, opts): signs and returns a compact JWT. verifySessionToken(jwt, relayerDID): resolves DID, verifies signature, validates nbf/exp window, and returns typed claims object. Exports BolyraSessionClaims interface.
Add BolyraSessionClaims interface: extends standard JWT claims with humanNullifier, agentNullifier, scopeCommitment, sessionNonce, bolyra_proof_tx fields. Add SessionTokenOptions type.

Unit tests: round-trip issue/verify, expired token rejection, nbf-future rejection, tampered claim rejection, missing bolyra_proof_tx rejection, scope commitment mismatch, and DID resolution failure path.
Integration guide for downstream HTTP services: how to validate a Bolyra JWT without on-chain calls, middleware snippet for Express/Fastify, and guidance on caching the relayer DID document.

steps: Define the BolyraSessionClaims schema: registered JWT claims (iss, sub, nbf, exp, jti) plus private claims (humanNullifier, agentNullifier, scopeCommitment, sessionNonce, bolyra_proof_tx). Namespace private claims under 'https://bolyra.ai/claims/' per RFC 7519 §4.3.

Write the normative spec in spec/session-token-format.md covering: claim semantics, mandatory vs optional fields, signing algorithm selection (ES256K preferred, EdDSA allowed), iss MUST be a did:bolyra: URI, exp MUST NOT exceed 1 hour, bolyra_proof_tx MUST be a valid tx hash on the configured chain.

Amend spec/did-method-bolyra.md to add a 'relayer' role with a verificationMethod of type EcdsaSecp256k1VerificationKey2019 or Ed25519VerificationKey2020, enabling downstream verifiers to resolve the public key from the iss DID.
Add BolyraSessionClaims and SessionTokenOptions types to sdk/src/types.ts.
Implement issueSessionToken() in sdk/src/session-token.ts: accept HandshakeResult + relayer private key, construct claims object, sign with jose (ES256K/EdDSA), return compact JWT string.

Implement verifySessionToken() in sdk/src/session-token.ts: parse and validate JWT header/signature using resolved relayer DID public key, enforce nbf <= now < exp, assert presence of all required Bolyra private claims, return typed BolyraSessionClaims.
Write unit tests in sdk/test/session-token.test.ts covering happy path, all rejection conditions, and boundary conditions on nbf/exp windows.
Export issueSessionToken, verifySessionToken, and BolyraSessionClaims from sdk/src/index.ts public API.
Write docs/session-token-quickstart.md with a middleware snippet showing how a downstream Express service validates the JWT using only verifySessionToken (no RPC call needed).
Run npm run typecheck and npm run test:contracts (no new circuits involved) to confirm no regressions.
dependencies: jose (JWT signing/verification library, JOSE RFC 7515/7519 compliant)
did:bolyra DID method resolution (spec/did-method-bolyra.md must define relayer verificationMethod)
verifyHandshake() returning HandshakeResult with humanNullifier, agentNullifier, scopeCommitment, sessionNonce fields
on-chain settlement tx hash (bolyra_proof_tx) available to the relayer at token issuance time

winner.title: Machine-readable conformance test vectors with deterministic fixtures
winner.description: Create a conformance/ directory with 30+ JSON test vectors organized by circuit (HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation) and scenario (valid, expired-credential, revoked-human, stale-root, scope-superset-rejection, cumulative-bit-violation, nonce-replay). Each vector includes deterministic private inputs, expected public outputs, and a 'should_pass' boolean. Use a fixed test secret/keypair so any implementation can reproduce identical commitments and nullifiers. This is the minimum bar for a credible multi-implementation ecosystem and should be referenced normatively from the IETF draft.

outline.title: Machine-readable conformance test vectors with deterministic fixtures

artifacts.description: Conformance suite overview: vector schema, fixed keypair derivation, how to run the validation harness, and normative reference pointer for the IETF draft
JSON Schema for a single test vector: privateInputs, publicOutputs, shouldPass, scenario, circuit, notes
Fixed deterministic test keypair: EdDSA private key (hex), public key (Bj, Bx), Poseidon-hash-based test secret, derived identityCommitment and trapdoor
10+ vectors for HumanUniqueness circuit: valid proof, stale-root, nonce-replay, revoked-human, wrong-trapdoor, tree-depth-mismatch
10+ vectors for AgentPolicy circuit: valid credential, expired-credential, cumulative-bit-violation (e.g. bit 3 set without bit 2), scope-superset-rejection, wrong-operator-sig
10+ vectors for Delegation circuit: valid narrowing, scope-superset-rejection (delegatee claims more than delegator), sub-delegate-without-bit-6, expired parent credential, double-delegation depth
5+ end-to-end handshake vectors combining HumanUniqueness + AgentPolicy + Delegation: full valid flow, mismatched sessionNonce, replay with stale nonce
Node script that uses @bolyra/sdk + snarkjs to recompute witnesses for every vector from its privateInputs and assert publicOutputs match; exits non-zero on mismatch
CI harness: loads all JSON vectors, validates against schema.json, runs witness generation (fast mode) for each, asserts shouldPass agreement; reports per-vector pass/fail table
How to add a new vector: deterministic input derivation rules, forbidden random values, PR checklist requiring schema validation and witness-gen pass
Amendment: add normative Section referencing conformance/ as the machine-readable test suite; link schema.json and keys.json as fixed anchors for interoperability

steps: Define and commit conformance/schema.json with required fields: id, circuit, scenario, privateInputs (object), publicOutputs (object), shouldPass (bool), notes (string)

Generate and commit conformance/fixtures/keys.json: derive all fixed values (EdDSA privkey, pubkey, Poseidon secret, identityCommitment, trapdoor) from a single seed constant using documented deterministic procedure

Author conformance/vectors/human_uniqueness.json with 10 vectors covering: valid, stale-merkle-root, nonce-replay, revoked-nullifier, wrong-trapdoor, tree-depth-1-vs-20, empty-leaf, all-zero-inputs, root-mismatch, double-spend-nullifier

Author conformance/vectors/agent_policy.json with 10 vectors covering: valid, expired-expiry, future-issuance, cumulative-bit-3-without-2, cumulative-bit-4-without-3, scope-superset (agent claims bit not in credential), wrong-operator-sig, zero-modelHash, mismatched-nonce, revoked-credential-hash

Author conformance/vectors/delegation.json with 10 vectors covering: valid-narrowing, scope-superset-rejection, sub-delegate-without-bit-6, expired-parent, zero-delegatee-pubkey, depth-exceeds-limit, parent-scope-zero, identity-delegation (no narrowing), cross-operator-delegation, nullifier-collision
Author conformance/vectors/cross_circuit.json with 5 end-to-end handshake vectors: full-valid-flow, mismatched-sessionNonce, stale-nonce-replay, human-not-in-tree, agent-scope-exceeds-human-grant

Write conformance/generate_fixtures.js: for each vector load privateInputs, call snarkjs.wtns.calculate() with appropriate circuit wasm, extract public signals, diff against publicOutputs, print structured results

Write conformance/validate.js: parse all JSON files in vectors/, validate each against schema.json using ajv, run witness generation in fast mode, assert shouldPass matches witness success/failure, exit non-zero on any mismatch
Add 'test:conformance' script to root package.json invoking node conformance/validate.js; wire into CI as a required check gate
Write conformance/README.md: explain fixed keypair rationale, vector schema, how to run validation, how to extend, and the normative link to the IETF draft
Write conformance/CONTRIBUTING.md: forbidden randomness rule (all entropy from documented seed), schema validation requirement, witness-gen pass requirement before PR merge
Amend spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md: add Section 9 'Conformance Test Vectors' with normative language referencing conformance/schema.json and keys.json as fixed anchors
Run full conformance suite (npm run test:conformance) and fix any witness mismatches in vector privateInputs or publicOutputs
Commit all artifacts with DCO sign-off; update CHANGELOG referencing the conformance suite addition

winner.title: Register application/bolyra-proof+cbor media type and define proof envelope
winner.description: The protocol currently has no standardized wire format for transmitting proofs between prover and verifier. Define a CBOR-based proof envelope (application/bolyra-proof+cbor) with a fixed schema: version (uint), circuit_id (text), proving_system (text: 'groth16'|'plonk'), public_signals (array<bstr>), proof (bstr), and optional delegation_chain (array<envelope>). Include a CDDL grammar in the IETF draft. This replaces ad-hoc JSON serialization in the SDK and enables language-agnostic interop — any conformant implementation can parse and forward proofs without SDK-specific knowledge.

outline.title: Register application/bolyra-proof+cbor media type and define proof envelope

artifacts.description: CDDL grammar defining the CBOR proof envelope schema: version, circuit_id, proving_system, public_signals, proof bstr, optional delegation_chain
Update existing IETF draft to include Section: Proof Envelope Format with CDDL grammar, media type registration template (RFC 6838), and Content-Type negotiation semantics
IANA media type registration template for application/bolyra-proof+cbor per RFC 6838: type name, subtype, required parameters, encoding, security considerations, contact
TypeScript module implementing ProofEnvelope encode/decode using cbor2 or cbor-x: serializeProofEnvelope(), deserializeProofEnvelope(), with full type definitions
Add ProofEnvelope, DelegationChain, ProvingSystem union type ('groth16' | 'plonk') to public SDK types
Re-export serializeProofEnvelope and deserializeProofEnvelope as part of public SDK surface
Unit tests: round-trip encode/decode for groth16 envelope, plonk envelope, envelope with delegation_chain, unknown version rejection, malformed bstr rejection
Language-agnostic conformance test vectors: hex-encoded CBOR bytes paired with decoded JSON representation for each envelope variant
Developer-facing guide: wire format rationale, Content-Type header usage, SDK encode/decode examples, interop notes for non-JS implementations

steps: Audit current SDK proof output: trace proveHandshake() and verifyHandshake() to identify all ad-hoc JSON serialization points and the exact fields emitted (proof object, publicSignals array, circuit metadata)

Define CDDL schema in spec/proof-envelope-cbor.cddl: version (uint, currently 1), circuit_id (tstr), proving_system (tstr .within ('groth16' / 'plonk')), public_signals (array of bstr), proof (bstr, snarkjs proof JSON encoded as CBOR bstr for forward compat), delegation_chain (optional array of recursive envelope)
Add ProofEnvelope and related types to sdk/src/types.ts; ensure ProvingSystem union matches circuit build outputs
Install cbor2 (or cbor-x) in sdk/package.json; verify license compatibility with Apache-2.0
Implement serializeProofEnvelope() in sdk/src/envelope.ts: map SDK proof output to CBOR map with defined key ordering, encode delegation_chain recursively
Implement deserializeProofEnvelope() with strict validation: reject unknown version, reject proving_system values outside the enum, enforce required fields before returning typed object

Wire envelope serialization into SDK public API: proveHandshake() returns ProofEnvelope; verifyHandshake() accepts ProofEnvelope; maintain backward-compat JSON path behind a flag for one release cycle

Generate hex-encoded conformance vectors for each envelope variant (groth16 no delegation, plonk no delegation, groth16 with one delegation level) and write to spec/conformance/proof-envelope-vectors.json
Write unit tests in sdk/test/envelope.test.ts covering round-trip, invalid version, invalid proving_system, missing required field, and delegation_chain recursion

Update spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md: add new section 'Proof Envelope Format' with embedded CDDL, Content-Type negotiation rules (Accept/Content-Type: application/bolyra-proof+cbor), and forward to IANA registration template

Draft spec/media-type-registration.md following RFC 6838 template: type name application, subtype bolyra-proof+cbor, structured syntax suffix +cbor per RFC 8949, encoding CBOR, security considerations (no executable content, proof bytes are opaque to parser), contact ZKProva Inc.
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md and docs/proof-envelope.md with Content-Type header examples, curl snippet, and interop note that any CBOR-capable runtime can parse without the SDK
Run npm run test:circuits:fast and npm run test:contracts to confirm no regressions; run sdk/test suite with new envelope tests
Bump sdk package version to 0.3.0 (minor: new public API surface) and update CHANGELOG
dependencies: cbor2 or cbor-x npm package (Apache-2.0 or MIT compatible)
snarkjs proof JSON schema (existing AgentPolicy and Delegation circuit outputs)
sdk/src/index.ts public API surface (to avoid breaking changes)

winner.title: Complete DID resolution algorithm with revocation and delegation discovery
winner.description: The existing did-method-bolyra.md spec defines the DID syntax but lacks a complete resolution algorithm. Specify the full CRUD lifecycle: Create (enrollment tx → DID Document with verificationMethod), Read (resolve commitment → on-chain lookup of enrollment event + current Merkle inclusion status), Update (not applicable — commitments are immutable), Deactivate (revocation nullifier check). Add a `delegationService` endpoint in the DID Document that points to the delegation chain discovery API. Include concrete JSON-LD examples for each operation and error codes for stale-root and revoked-identity resolution failures.

outline.title: Complete DID resolution algorithm with revocation and delegation discovery

artifacts.description: Extend existing DID method spec with full CRUD lifecycle: Create (enrollment tx → DID Document), Read (resolve commitment → on-chain lookup + Merkle inclusion), Update (immutable — explicit non-operation), Deactivate (revocation nullifier check). Add resolution algorithm pseudocode, error codes (STALE_ROOT, REVOKED_IDENTITY, NOT_FOUND, INVALID_COMMITMENT), and delegationService endpoint definition.
Standalone resolution algorithm document: input/output types, step-by-step resolver logic, caching policy for Merkle roots, conformance requirements per W3C DID Core §7.

Concrete JSON-LD DID Document examples for each lifecycle operation: freshly enrolled human identity, resolved agent credential DID, deactivated (revoked) identity with nullifier proof, and DID Document with delegationService endpoint.
Error code registry: invalidDid, notFound, deactivated (maps to revocationNullifierFound), representationNotSupported, staleRoot (Merkle root lag > threshold), delegationChainBroken.

TypeScript DID resolver implementing the algorithm: resolveHuman(commitment) and resolveAgent(credentialHash) returning W3C DID Resolution Result objects, with on-chain EnrollmentRegistry lookups, nullifier checks, and delegationService URL construction.
DID Document type definitions: DidDocument, VerificationMethod (Ed25519 + BLS12-381), ServiceEndpoint, DelegationService, ResolutionMetadata, DocumentMetadata.

On-chain registry emitting EnrollmentRegistered and IdentityDeactivated events with indexed commitment fields to support resolution queries. Exposes currentMerkleRoot() and isNullifierRevoked(bytes32) view functions.

Conformance test suite validating the resolver against all W3C DID Core resolution algorithm requirements: happy path, stale-root error, revoked identity error, delegation chain discovery, and representation negotiation.
Unit tests for the TypeScript resolver: mock EnrollmentRegistry, nullifier state, and Merkle inclusion proofs. Covers all error code paths.

steps: Audit existing spec/did-method-bolyra.md to inventory what syntax and DID Document structure is already defined; note gaps in CRUD lifecycle coverage
Define the canonical DID syntax: did:bolyra:<network>:<commitment-hash> for humans, did:bolyra:<network>:agent:<credential-hash> for agents

Specify Create operation: map enrollment transaction (EnrollmentRegistry.enroll(commitment)) to DID Document with Ed25519 verificationMethod derived from commitment, plus Merkle inclusion proof as documentMetadata

Specify Read (resolve) operation: (1) parse DID → extract commitment/credential hash, (2) query EnrollmentRegistry for EnrollmentRegistered event, (3) verify current Merkle inclusion via inclusion proof or on-chain root, (4) check nullifier registry for deactivation, (5) construct DID Document with current verificationMethods and serviceEndpoints
Specify Update operation explicitly as NOT SUPPORTED with rationale: commitments are cryptographic commitments, immutable by design; key rotation requires new enrollment and DID

Specify Deactivate operation: submitting a valid revocation nullifier to NullifierRegistry marks the DID as deactivated; resolver MUST return deactivated=true in documentMetadata and error code 'deactivated'

Define delegationService endpoint schema: type='BolyraD elegationChain', serviceEndpoint pointing to delegation discovery API with did parameter; describe how resolver populates this from on-chain delegation events
Define error codes and map to W3C DID resolution error vocabulary: invalidDid, notFound, deactivated, staleRoot (configurable lag threshold), delegationChainBroken, representationNotSupported
Write concrete JSON-LD examples for all operations and embed in spec/did-resolution-examples.jsonld with @context referencing did-bolyra context document
Implement DID Document types in sdk/src/did/types.ts aligned with W3C DID Core data model

Implement resolver in sdk/src/did/resolver.ts: resolveHuman and resolveAgent functions, ethers.js queries against DIDRegistry and NullifierRegistry, Merkle inclusion verification via snarkjs witness check

Add DIDRegistry.sol with EnrollmentRegistered(bytes32 indexed commitment, address enrollee, uint256 timestamp) and IdentityDeactivated(bytes32 indexed nullifier) events plus view functions currentMerkleRoot() and isNullifierRevoked(bytes32)
Write conformance test suite covering all W3C DID Core §7 resolution algorithm steps
Write SDK unit tests mocking on-chain state for all error paths
Update spec/did-method-bolyra.md to reference the new resolution algorithm document and examples
Cross-reference FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md to ensure resolver's nullifier check aligns with circuit-enforced nullifier domain separation
dependencies: circuits/src/HumanUniqueness.circom (nullifierHash output used as deactivation key)
circuits/src/AgentPolicy.circom (credentialHash used as agent DID identifier)
W3C DID Resolution spec (resolutionMetadata + documentMetadata types)
snarkjs (Merkle inclusion witness verification in resolver)
ethers.js v6 (on-chain event and view function queries)

winner.title: Structured error codes with recovery hints
winner.description: SDK currently throws generic Error('proof verification failed'). Introduce a BolyraError class with machine-readable codes (STALE_ROOT, EXPIRED_CREDENTIAL, SCOPE_MISMATCH, NONCE_REUSED, NULLIFIER_SPENT) and a `.hint` field with the specific fix ('Root is 3 blocks behind; re-fetch with registry.latestRoot()'). Map on-chain revert reasons from IdentityRegistry's custom errors to SDK-level diagnostics. This is the single biggest DX gap — developers can't debug failures without reading circuit code.

outline.title: Structured error codes with recovery hints

artifacts.description: BolyraError class with code enum (STALE_ROOT, EXPIRED_CREDENTIAL, SCOPE_MISMATCH, NONCE_REUSED, NULLIFIER_SPENT, PROOF_INVALID, REGISTRY_REVERT, UNKNOWN), hint string, and optional cause chaining
Exported ErrorCode enum and HintMap mapping each code to a template hint string with interpolation placeholders
mapRevertToBolyraError() — decodes ethers ContractErrorData / revert reason strings from IdentityRegistry custom errors into BolyraError instances
Updated verifyHandshake() and verifyProof() to catch snarkjs/rapidsnark failures and rethrow as typed BolyraError with contextual hint
Audit and enumerate existing custom errors; add any missing ones (StaleRoot, NullifierSpent, ScopeMismatch) with parameters for SDK-side interpolation
Normative list of all BolyraError codes, their trigger conditions, and canonical hint templates for SDK implementors
Unit tests: each error code thrown under its trigger condition, hint contains expected substring, cause chain preserved, revert decoder maps every IdentityRegistry custom error
Add error-handling section showing try/catch on BolyraError, code switch, and acting on .hint

steps: Audit IdentityRegistry.sol for all existing custom errors; document their parameter types and add StaleRoot(uint256 providedBlock, uint256 latestBlock), NullifierSpent(bytes32 nullifier), and ScopeMismatch(uint8 required, uint8 provided) if missing
Define ErrorCode enum in error-codes.ts with values: STALE_ROOT, EXPIRED_CREDENTIAL, SCOPE_MISMATCH, NONCE_REUSED, NULLIFIER_SPENT, PROOF_INVALID, REGISTRY_REVERT, UNKNOWN
Implement HintMap in error-codes.ts mapping each ErrorCode to a hint template string (e.g., STALE_ROOT => 'Root is {delta} blocks behind; re-fetch with registry.latestRoot()')

Implement BolyraError extends Error in errors.ts with fields: code: ErrorCode, hint: string, cause?: unknown; include static factory helpers (BolyraError.staleRoot(delta), etc.) that interpolate hint templates

Implement revert-decoder.ts: parse ethers Interface.parseError() output against IdentityRegistry ABI fragments, map each custom error selector to the corresponding BolyraError factory; fall back to REGISTRY_REVERT for unknown reverts
Wrap snarkjs/rapidsnark verification calls in proof-verifier.ts: catch witness generation failures as PROOF_INVALID, expired credential detection as EXPIRED_CREDENTIAL, nonce mismatch as NONCE_REUSED
Update verifyHandshake() public entrypoint to catch all thrown errors and rethrow as BolyraError if not already typed, preserving cause chain

Write unit tests in errors.test.ts covering: each code triggered under its condition, hint interpolation correctness, revert decoder for every IdentityRegistry custom error selector, cause chain preservation
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md with a try/catch example demonstrating BolyraError code switching and acting on .hint
Export BolyraError and ErrorCode from sdk/src/index.ts public surface
Update spec/error-codes.md with normative definitions of all codes, trigger conditions, and hint templates
dependencies: ethers v6 Interface.parseError() for ABI-level revert decoding
IdentityRegistry.sol ABI with custom error definitions
snarkjs proof verification error surface (must enumerate failure modes)

winner.title: LangChain credential middleware with automatic scope-gated tool access
winner.description: Build a LangChain CallbackHandler + BaseTool wrapper that intercepts tool invocations and requires a valid Bolyra AgentPolicy proof before execution. The middleware maps LangChain tool names to permission bitmask bits (e.g., SQLDatabaseTool → READ_DATA, StripeChargeTool → FINANCIAL_SMALL) via a declarative YAML config. On first call, it runs proveHandshake() and caches the proof for the session nonce lifetime. Publish as @bolyra/langchain on npm and bolyra-langchain on PyPI, with a 5-line quickstart that wraps an existing AgentExecutor.

outline.title: LangChain credential middleware with automatic scope-gated tool access

artifacts.description: LangChain CallbackHandler that intercepts onToolStart events, validates cached AgentPolicy proof against required permission bitmask, and blocks execution if scope is insufficient
BaseTool wrapper factory that decorates any LangChain tool with scope annotation and delegates enforcement to BolyraCallbackHandler
Default tool-name to permission bitmask mapping (SQLDatabaseTool → READ_DATA, StripeChargeTool → FINANCIAL_SMALL, etc.) with merge support for user-supplied overrides
In-memory LRU cache keyed by sessionNonce that stores proveHandshake() results and evicts on nonce expiry
Public barrel export: BolyraCallbackHandler, wrapTools(), loadToolPermissionConfig()
JSON Schema (expressed in YAML) for the declarative tool→bitmask config file; validated at middleware init
Reference YAML config mapping common LangChain community tools to Bolyra permission bits
Python port: BaseCallbackHandler subclass + wrap_tools() helper; proving delegates to @bolyra/sdk subprocess bridge
Unit tests: proof cache hit/miss, scope gate blocks under-privileged calls, YAML config loading, nonce expiry eviction
Integration test: full AgentExecutor run with mock LLM, real proveHandshake() (fast/mock proof), verifies blocked vs allowed tool paths
Python equivalent integration test using pytest and langchain-community stubs
5-line quickstart wrapping an existing AgentExecutor, YAML config reference, permission bit table, nonce lifetime notes
npm publish (@bolyra/langchain) and PyPI publish (bolyra-langchain) runbook, peer dependency matrix, versioning policy

steps: Define tool-permissions.schema.yaml with ajv-compatible JSON Schema; list all 8 permission bit names as enum values
Implement toolPermissionMap.ts with hardcoded defaults for top-10 LangChain community tools and a mergePermissionConfig() that deep-merges user YAML
Implement ProofSessionCache.ts: Map<sessionNonce, {proof, expiresAt}>; add get/set/evict; wire eviction to AgentPolicy expiry field

Implement BolyraCallbackHandler.ts: onToolStart() resolves tool name → required bitmask via config, checks cache for valid proof, calls proveHandshake() on miss, throws BolyraInsufficientScopeError if proof bitmask & required !== required
Implement BolyraToolWrapper.ts: wrapTools(tools[], config) annotates each tool with requiredPermission metadata and returns tools unchanged (handler does enforcement — no monkey-patching call())
Wire index.ts barrel; add package.json for @bolyra/langchain with peerDeps on @bolyra/sdk ^0.2.0 and @langchain/core ^0.2.0
Write unit tests for cache logic, scope gate arithmetic, and YAML loading; mock proveHandshake()
Write integration test: construct AgentExecutor with BolyraCallbackHandler, run agent with mock LLM that forces a FINANCIAL_SMALL tool call, assert blocked when credential has only READ_DATA
Port to Python: implement bolyra_langchain/handler.py as BaseCallbackHandler, delegate proof to subprocess bridge (same pattern as sdk-python), publish bolyra-langchain to PyPI
Write Python pytest integration test mirroring the TS integration test
Draft README.md with 5-line quickstart, full YAML config reference, and security notes on nonce lifetime
Add @bolyra/langchain to root package.json workspaces; update landing page ecosystem section

winner.title: Standardized proof envelope with application/bolyra+json content type
winner.description: Define a JSON envelope format for proof payloads that all framework integrations use: {version, circuit, publicSignals[], proof{pi_a, pi_b, pi_c}, metadata{framework, sdkVersion, provingTimeMs}}. Register application/bolyra+json as the content type. This lets any HTTP-based agent framework (MCP servers, OpenAI function calling proxies, REST tool servers) accept Bolyra proofs via a standard Authorization header (Bearer bolyra:<base64-envelope>) without framework-specific code. Include a generic Express/FastAPI middleware that validates the header, so tool servers get ZK auth in 3 lines.

outline.title: Standardized proof envelope with application/bolyra+json content type

artifacts.description: Formal specification of the application/bolyra+json envelope schema: version, circuit name, publicSignals array, proof object (pi_a/pi_b/pi_c), and metadata block. Includes ABNF for the Authorization header value 'Bearer bolyra:<base64url-envelope>'.
IANA-style registration template for application/bolyra+json: type, subtype, required fields, optional fields, encoding considerations, security considerations.
TypeScript implementation of ProofEnvelope type, serializeEnvelope(), deserializeEnvelope(), and encodeAuthHeader() / decodeAuthHeader() utilities. Validates schema on both encode and decode paths.

Express middleware: extractBolyraProof(req, res, next) — parses Authorization header, base64url-decodes envelope, validates schema, calls verifyHandshake(), attaches result to req.bolyraIdentity. 3-line usage: app.use(extractBolyraProof).

FastAPI dependency: bolyra_auth = Depends(BolyraAuthMiddleware()) — parses Authorization header, delegates proof verification to the Node subprocess bridge, returns BolyraIdentity pydantic model. 3-line usage via Depends().

Unit tests for round-trip serialize/deserialize, Authorization header encode/decode, schema validation rejections (missing fields, wrong version, malformed base64), and Express middleware integration with a mock Express app.
pytest tests for FastAPI middleware: valid proof header, missing header 401, malformed base64 400, expired credential 403. Uses httpx TestClient.

Integration guide showing 3-line Express setup, 3-line FastAPI setup, curl example with a real base64url-encoded envelope, and a table mapping framework types (MCP server, OpenAI function proxy, REST tool server) to the middleware import path.

steps: Define ProofEnvelopeV1 JSON schema in spec/proof-envelope-v1.md: required fields version (string semver), circuit (string enum: HumanUniqueness|AgentPolicy|Delegation), publicSignals (string[] hex), proof ({pi_a: string[], pi_b: string[][], pi_c: string[]}), metadata ({framework?: string, sdkVersion: string, provingTimeMs?: number})
Write serializeEnvelope() and deserializeEnvelope() in sdk/src/envelope.ts with zod schema validation; export ProofEnvelope TypeScript type
Implement encodeAuthHeader(envelope: ProofEnvelope): string (returns 'bolyra <base64url>') and decodeAuthHeader(header: string): ProofEnvelope with error throws for malformed input

Build Express middleware extractBolyraProof in sdk/src/middleware/express.ts: parse header, decode envelope, call verifyHandshake() from existing SDK, attach to req.bolyraIdentity, call next() on success or return 401/403 JSON error

Build FastAPI dependency BolyraAuthMiddleware in sdk-python/bolyra/middleware/fastapi.py: extract Authorization header via Request, delegate to subprocess bridge for verification, return BolyraIdentity pydantic model or raise HTTPException
Write unit tests for envelope serialization round-trips, header encode/decode, and schema rejection cases in sdk/test/envelope.test.ts
Write pytest tests for FastAPI middleware covering valid, missing, malformed, and expired cases in sdk-python/tests/test_middleware_fastapi.py
Update sdk/src/index.ts to export serializeEnvelope, deserializeEnvelope, encodeAuthHeader, decodeAuthHeader, and extractBolyraProof
Update sdk-python/bolyra/__init__.py to expose BolyraAuthMiddleware
Write spec/content-type-registration.md following IANA structured syntax template, referencing proof-envelope-v1.md for schema
Write docs/proof-envelope-quickstart.md with Express snippet, FastAPI snippet, curl example, and framework compatibility table
Run npm run typecheck in sdk/ and pytest in sdk-python/ to confirm no regressions; verify existing verifyHandshake integration tests still pass
dependencies: sdk-python subprocess bridge to Node for proof verification
zod (already in sdk/package.json) for schema validation
fastapi + httpx (already in sdk-python) for middleware and tests

winner.title: Off-chain JWT session token from verified handshake for framework middleware
winner.description: After a successful on-chain verifyHandshake(), the SDK should emit a signed JWT containing the nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, and sessionNonce with a configurable TTL (default 5 min). Framework middleware (LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen) can then verify this JWT locally for subsequent tool calls in the same session without re-proving on-chain. This eliminates the biggest adoption blocker: multi-second proof generation on every tool invocation. The JWT issuer key is derived from the agent's operator EdDSA key, so verification stays self-sovereign.

outline.title: Off-chain JWT session token from verified handshake for framework middleware

artifacts.description: Core SessionToken class: issueSessionJWT(handshakeResult, operatorEdDSAKey, ttlSeconds) derives a JWT signing key from the operator EdDSA key via HKDF, embeds nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, sessionNonce, iat, exp, and iss claims, signs with HS256 or EdDSA (Ed25519), and returns a compact JWT string.
verifySessionJWT(token, operatorPublicKey): reconstructs the expected signing key, verifies signature and expiry, returns typed SessionClaims or throws SessionExpiredError / SessionInvalidError.
Add SessionClaims interface (nullifierHash, scopeCommitment, sessionNonce, iat, exp, iss) and SessionTokenOptions (ttlSeconds, clockSkewSeconds).
Re-export issueSessionJWT, verifySessionJWT, SessionClaims, SessionTokenOptions from the public API surface.
LangChain StructuredTool wrapper that checks a session JWT on every tool invocation using verifySessionJWT; injects SessionClaims into tool input context. Throws if token is absent or expired.
LangChain BaseCallbackHandler that stores the session JWT in async-local-storage at chain start and clears it on chain end, enabling middleware to resolve the token without explicit passing.
CrewAI tool decorator @require_bolyra_session that calls the Python SDK subprocess bridge to verify the JWT before the tool function executes. Raises PermissionError on failure.

Specification for the JWT payload schema, key derivation (HKDF-SHA256 from operator EdDSA private scalar), signing algorithm selection (Ed25519 preferred, HS256 fallback), replay-prevention via sessionNonce uniqueness, and TTL guidance. Defines the relationship between on-chain verifyHandshake() and off-chain JWT issuance as a trust delegation boundary.
Unit tests: issue → verify round-trip, expired token rejection, tampered claim rejection, wrong operator key rejection, scopeCommitment mismatch, clock skew tolerance, default TTL=300s enforcement.
Integration test: runs full proveHandshake() + verifyHandshake() mock, calls issueSessionJWT on the result, verifies the JWT, confirms nullifierHash in claims matches the proof public output.
LangChain middleware tests: tool call with valid JWT succeeds, tool call with missing JWT throws, tool call with expired JWT throws, sessionNonce is propagated into tool context.

Developer guide: 3-step integration (run handshake, issue JWT, protect tools). Includes copy-paste snippets for LangChain, CrewAI, and raw verifySessionJWT usage. Documents TTL tradeoffs and key rotation guidance.

steps: Define SessionClaims interface and SessionTokenOptions in sdk/src/types.ts with nullifierHash (hex string), scopeCommitment (hex string), sessionNonce (hex string), iat, exp, iss fields
Implement HKDF-SHA256 key derivation in sdk/src/session.ts: take operator EdDSA private scalar bytes as IKM, use info='bolyra-session-jwt-v1' and salt=nullifierHash to produce a 32-byte symmetric key

Implement issueSessionJWT(): validate handshakeResult fields are present, derive signing key via HKDF, build JWT payload with claims + exp=iat+ttlSeconds, sign with jose or jsonwebtoken library using HS256, return compact JWT

Implement verifySessionJWT(): derive verification key from operatorPublicKey (symmetric key derivation requires private key — document that verification is operator-side only OR switch to Ed25519 asymmetric JWT for public verifiability; pick asymmetric Ed25519 for self-sovereign property stated in candidate)

Revise signing to use Ed25519 asymmetric JWT (alg: EdDSA): operator EdDSA private key signs JWT directly, verifier uses operator public key — aligns with 'verification stays self-sovereign' requirement
Update issueSessionJWT() to sign with Ed25519 private key via jose library; update verifySessionJWT() to verify with Ed25519 public key
Write unit tests in sdk/test/session.test.ts covering round-trip, expiry, tampering, wrong key, and clock skew
Write integration test in sdk/test/session-integration.test.ts connecting mock handshake output to JWT issuance and verification
Re-export issueSessionJWT, verifySessionJWT, SessionClaims, SessionTokenOptions from sdk/src/index.ts
Implement LangChain StructuredTool middleware in integrations/langchain/src/bolyra-tool-middleware.ts using verifySessionJWT on each invocation
Implement LangChain BaseCallbackHandler for async-local-storage session propagation in bolyra-session-callback.ts
Write LangChain middleware tests in integrations/langchain/test/
Implement CrewAI @require_bolyra_session decorator in integrations/crewai/bolyra_session.py calling Python SDK subprocess bridge for JWT verification
Write spec/session-token-spec.md documenting JWT schema, key derivation rationale, trust boundary, replay prevention via sessionNonce, and TTL guidance
Write docs/session-token-quickstart.md with LangChain, CrewAI, and raw API snippets
Run npm run typecheck and npm test to confirm no regressions
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md to reference session token as the recommended pattern for multi-step agent sessions
dependencies: jose (^5.x) — Ed25519 JWT sign/verify in Node
Existing proveHandshake() and verifyHandshake() in sdk/src/index.ts
Operator EdDSA key pair already produced by createAgentCredential()
Python SDK subprocess bridge (sdk-python/bolyra/bridge.py) for CrewAI integration
sessionNonce uniqueness guarantee from handshake flow (replay prevention)
